en.extract:
The dusky gerygone (Gerygone tenebrosa) is a species of bird in the family Acanthizidae.
It is endemic to coastal central and northern areas of Western Australia, from Shark Bay to Kunmuya.
Its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical mangrove forests.


Description
The dusky gerygone is about 10-13 cm in length, with the upper parts grey-brown and tinged olive. The underparts are a greyish white, legs and beak are black.

The '''dusky gerygone''' ('''''Gerygone tenebrosa''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Acanthizidae]].
It is [[Endemism|endemic]] to coastal central and northern areas of [[Western Australia]], from Shark Bay to Kunmuya.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=Dusky Gerygone |url=https://museum.wa.gov.au/online-collections/names/gerygone-tenebrosa |website=museum.wa.gov.au}}</ref>

Its natural [[habitat]] is subtropical or tropical mangrove [[forest]]s.<ref name=":0" />

== Description ==
The dusky gerygone is about 10-13 cm in length, with the upper parts grey-brown and tinged olive. The underparts are a greyish white, legs and beak are black.<ref name=":0" />
